quoteFirst: General Hazen reached the vicinity of Fort McAllister about 1 P. M., deployed his division about that place, with both flanks resting upon the river, posted his skirmishers judiciously behind the trunks of trees whose branches had been used for _abattis_, and
quoteLast: , assaulted the place with nine regiments at three points; all of them successful.
title:
Life of Wm. Tecumseh Sherman.
Late Retired General. U. S. A.
author: Johnson, Willis Fletcher, 1857-1931; Howard, O. O. (Oliver Otis), 1830-1909 [Author of introduction, etc.]

quoteLast: we saw 22 of the native boats or canoes going along shore to the westwards, on which we suspected some treachery; wherefore on the 12th we made sail farther along the coast eastwards, and descried more towns, in which there were some larger houses than any we had hitherto seen, and from these the people came out to look at us, but we could see no boats on the shore.
title: A General History and Collection of Voyages and Travels — Volume 07
author: Kerr, Robert, 1755-1813

quoteFirst: We marched about four P.M., and with only short rests     all night and all next day till
quoteLast: , when we halted to     rest and bivouac at a point which I cannot remember; but our cavalry     had had a skirmish there with the enemy's cavalry just before our     arrival, and I remember seeing some killed and wounded of each side.
title: From Manassas to Appomattox: Memoirs of the Civil War in America
author: Longstreet, James, 1821-1904

quoteFirst: Mr Devas, who had sustained a severe fracture of the skull, was brought back to the Riffel-Haus
quoteLast: , where he received the most unremitting care from M. Seiler's staff of servants.
title: Adventures on the Roof of the World
author: Le Blond, Aubrey, Mrs., 1861-1934

quoteTime: about five o’clock in the afternoon
quoteLast: he awoke with a sudden start, ate a bit of luncheon, and then set to work cleaning his gun, being thus and in other ways engaged until nearly ten o’clock, when without a word he shouldered his weapon and started out of the house.
title: Catherine's coquetries
author: Debans, Camille, 1832-1919; Mead, Leon, 1861-1927 [Translator]

quoteLast: the 167th Brigade again retired to a more favourable position, which gave them a net gain of 400 yards beyond their original line.
title: The 56th Division (1st London Territorial Division)
author: Dudley Ward, C. H. (Charles Humble), 1879-1945; Horne, Henry, 1861-1929 [Author of introduction, etc.]

quoteLast: , I received a most unexpected message from General Joffre by telegraph, telling me that at least three German corps, viz.
title: World's War Events, Vol. I
author: Reynolds, Francis J. (Francis Joseph), 1867-1937 [Editor]; Churchill, Allen L. (Allen Leon), 1873-1940 [Editor]

quoteLast: , and massed in battle order on the bluffs near the river.
title: Slavery and four years of war
author: Keifer, Joseph Warren, 1836-1932

quoteTime: About five o'clock in the afternoon
quoteLast: we took our departure with not the most pleasant feelings.
title: The Rise and Fall of Nauvoo
